provenance.source_corpus: Reconnecting Amphibian Habitat through Small Pond Construction and Enhancement, South Okanagan River Valley, British Columbia, Canada

source_spans.text: Habitat loss is considered the most significant factor in this decline
amphibian populations are globally threatened... implying a rate of mass extinction... Habitat loss is considered the most significant factor in this decline
Wetland habitat construction and enhancement have doubled the number of available fishless ponds
Wetland habitat construction and enhancement have doubled the number of available fishless ponds
We have evidence of amphibian colonization at 90% of our ponds
with confirmed metamorphosis at 62% of ponds
Successful removal of non-native invasive goldfish (Carassius auratus) at Site 14 resulted in rapid colonization
Successful removal of non-native invasive goldfish (Carassius auratus) at Site 14 resulted in rapid colonization of both frog species
Permanent ponds resulted in about twice the likelihood of metamorphic success
Ephemeral ponds with metamorphic success was likely a result of supplemental irrigation water
Intense agricultural practices (high inputs, monocrop culture, soil compaction) and urbanization are the mechanisms driving the loss and transformation of critical native desert habitat
Intense agricultural practices (high inputs, monocrop culture, soil compaction) and urbanization are the mechanisms driving the loss and transformation of critical native desert habitat
soil compaction... likely impedes fossorial species movement through agricultural and urban developed lands
Where eggs and/or tadpoles were detected, but not metamorphs, it was presumed that predation was a likely contributor
The association of predatory fish with reduced species richness... is well acknowledged in the literature
The association of predatory fish with reduced species richness... is well acknowledged in the literature
